question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: Transaction (field: chargedTxFee + highVolumePricingMultiplier)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where chargedTxFee is inconsistent with the expected fee after applying highVolumePricingMultiplier, given no cross-field validation enforces fee = baseFee * (highVolumePricingMultiplier / 1000), causing the mirror node to store fee data that contradicts the HIP-1313 pricing model?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: Transaction (field: maxCustomFees)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with maxCustomFees entries where the account_id references the same account as payerAccountId, given no self-reference check exists, causing the mirror node to store fee limit records where the payer is also the fee limit setter, potentially bypassing custom fee enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: Transaction (field: maxCustomFees)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with duplicate maxCustomFees entries for the same account_id and denominating_token_id, given no deduplication check exists in the domain model, causing the mirror node to store redundant fee limit records that could be exploited to confuse fee cap enforcement?
